channel.title: Drug Empire: Cartels & Kingpins in 60 Seconds
channel.description: The rise and fall of the world's most powerful drug lords — narcos, mafias and kingpins in 60 seconds.
channel.itunes:author: Drug Empire
channel.itunes:summary: The rise and fall of the world's most powerful drug lords — narcos, mafias and kingpins in 60 seconds.

item.title: Sicilian Mafia
item.description: The Sicilian Mafia or Cosa Nostra (Italian: [ˈkɔːza ˈnɔstra, ˈkɔːsa -]; Sicilian: [ˈkɔːsa ˈnɔʂː(ɽ)a]; lit. 'Our Thing'), also simply referred to as the Mafia, is an Italian criminal society and criminal organization originating on the island of Sicily and dating back to the mid-19th century. Emerging as a form of local protection and control over land and agriculture, the Mafia gradually evolved i

item.title: Frank Costello
item.description: Frank Costello (Italian: [koˈstɛllo]; born Francesco Castiglia [franˈtʃesko kaˈstiʎʎa]; January 26, 1891 – February 18, 1973) was an Italian-American crime boss of the Luciano crime family. Born in Italy, he moved with his family to the United States as a child. As a youth he joined New York City gangs. Working with Charlie "Lucky" Luciano, Costello was involved in bootlegging operations during Pr

item.title: Chicago Outfit
item.description: The Chicago Outfit, also known as the Outfit, the Chicago Mafia, the Chicago Mob, the Chicago crime family, the South Side Gang or the Organization, is an Italian American Mafia crime family based in Chicago, Illinois, and throughout the Greater Chicago area, originating in the city's South Side in the early 1910s. The Outfit rose to power in the 1920s under the control of Johnny Torrio and Al Cap

item.title: 2011 Monterrey casino attack
item.description: On August 25, 2011, members of the drug cartel Los Zetas set a casino on fire in Monterrey, Nuevo León, Mexico, killing 52 people. The arson attack left over a dozen injured, and over 35 trapped for several hours. Mexican forces, which arrived minutes after the incident, eventually used backhoes to break down the walls and free the trapped victims. Media reports state the majority of those killed
